name: ingen-lib

metadata.name: ingen-lib
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A Python script suite that generates interface files based on the given interface metadata/config file

documentation: InGen is a command line tool written on top of pandas and great_expectations to perform small scale data transformations and validations without writing code. It is designed for developers and analysts to quickly transform data by specifying their requirements in a simple YAML file.

classes.name: ingen.metadata.metadata_parser.MetaDataParser

methods.name: ingen.metadata.metadata_parser.MetaDataParser.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a metadata parser

Args:
    filepath: Metadata file path
    query_params: Key Value pairs passed from CLI that is used to replace keys with values in sql queries
    run_date: A date param to determine the date on which InGen runs, defaults to current date
    selected_interfaces: List of interface names. This should be a subset of names declared in the metadata file
    infile: A file path passed from command line to load a File Source
    dynamic_data: JSON String passed from command line to load a JSON Source

methods.name: ingen.metadata.metadata_parser.MetaDataParser.parse_metadata
documentation:
Parse the YAML metadata file

```
return

A list of interface MetaData objects
```
MetaDataParser reads a YAML file and creates a MetaData object for each interface

methods.name: ingen.reader.api_reader.APIReader.execute
documentation:
Method that return the required dataframe from a list of urls(by appending the result from each url).
param data_node: List of required nodes(of type list) from the api
param data_key: List of keys (of type dictionary/string) from the api
param meta: List of meta fields to be read from the response (see meta arg of pandas.json_normalize())

classes.name: ingen.metadata.metadata.MetaData

methods.name: ingen.metadata.metadata.MetaData.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a MetaData object for an interface

Args:
    name: Name of the interface
    configurations: A dictionary representing the properties of the interface
    params_map: A dictionary containing command line parameters: query_params, run_date and infile
    dynamic_data: JSON String passed from command line to load a JSON Source

This class represents the metadata of a single interface file. It includes information
like column details, source(s), output file name and type, etc.

classes.name: ingen.reader.rawdata_reader.RawDataReader
documentation:
A class written to read specific input IDs and read the Dataframe stored corresponding to that ID in the Store
Dictionary

modules.name: ingen.reader.json_reader
documentation: This script convert json file to csv file

modules.name: ingen
documentation: Import these attributes into other files as needed to
identify the project version at runtime.

methods.name: ingen.lib.cryptor.Cryptor.__fetch_key
documentation:
Method to fetch Keys from the sybase db using the DSREAD access.
\:param key_name: Name of the key at this path
\:param key_version: key version, if not provided latest version will be returned
\:return: String kev value.

methods.name: ingen.lib.cryptor.Cryptor.__get_hmac_key
documentation:
Placeholder method to get the HMAC key from the KMS.
\:return: Key Bytes

methods.name: ingen.lib.cryptor.Cryptor.__get_key
documentation:
Placeholder method to get the AES key from the KMS.
\:return: Key Bytes

methods.name: ingen.lib.cryptor.Cryptor.encrypt
documentation:
Encryption method using the AES/CTR/NoPadding.
While encrypting we always want to use the latest key
hence we pass the key_version as none.
\:param plaintext: The message to be encrypted.
\:return: JSON of format cipher and nonce.

methods.name: ingen.lib.cryptor.Cryptor.decrypt
documentation:
The Decryption method to take the JSON object of type Ciper and Nonce.
\:param message: JSON Object.
\:return: String message.

classes.name: ingen.pre_processor.process.Process

methods.name: ingen.pre_processor.process.Process.execute
documentation:
Method responsible for executing the processing step on data
\:param config: configuration to use for merging the data
\:param sources_data: array of data from all sources
\:param data: pre-processed data till now
\:return: A Pandas DataFrame with the result of the process

A Process represents a process to be applied on the dataframe(s). All process must implement
a method called execute, which is responsible for processing and returning data and returning a processed dataframe

functions.name: ingen.pre_processor.aggregators.groupby
documentation:
groups data by configured column

:param config : columns for grouping
    config columns on which group ing has to be performed as expressed below
    {'cols': ['acc', 'cusip']}
    the intent here is to group on columns acc, cusip
:param data : data to be grouped

functions.name: ingen.pre_processor.aggregators.agg
documentation:
aggregates passed data as per configuration

:param config : configuration for aggregator
    config consist of the operation to be
    performed and the columns on which the operation has to be performed.
    example of config : {'operation': 'sum', 'col': 'quantity'}
    once the aggregation is applied (example sum(quantity) here)
:param data : data to be aggregated

functions.name: ingen.pre_processor.aggregators.get_aggregator
documentation:
gets an aggregator, returns aggregator function

:param aggregator_type : name/type of the aggregator

functions.name: ingen.pre_processor.aggregators.add_aggregator
documentation:
adds a new aggregator

:param aggregator_type : name/type of the aggregator
:param aggregator : aggregator function

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.column_filter
documentation:
Remove columns from dataframe that are not required
\:param dataframe: Dataframe
\:param required_column_names: List of required column names
\:return: Dataframe with only required columns in the desired order

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.encryption_formatter
documentation:
Apply the encryption UDF on the selected column.
\:param dataframe: Input Data frame.
\:param col_id: The column to be encrypted
\:param format_options: Not required, but to be kept to not break the framework.
\:param runtime_params: Not required, but to be kept to not break the framework.
\:return:

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.decryption_formatter
documentation:
Apply the decryption UDF on the selected column.
\:param dataframe: Input Data frame.
\:param col_id: The column to be decrypted
\:param format_options: Not required, but to be kept to not break the framework.
\:param runtime_params: Not required, but to be kept to not break the framework.
\:return:

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.concat_formatter
documentation:
Concatenates the provided columns and adds as a new column to the dataframe
\:param dataframe: original data frame
\:param col_name: name of the new column
\:param columns: list of names of columns to be concatenated
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: dataframe with a new concatenated column

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.duplicate_column_formatter
documentation:
Adds a duplicate column with a new name
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param duplicate_col_name: name to be given to the new column
\:param original_col_name: name of the column that is duplicated
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: dataframe with a duplicate column

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.group_percentage_formatter
documentation:
Adds a new column with the percentage of column A after grouping the dataframe by column B
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: name of the new column
\:param format_options: name of column A and B in dictionary format.
e.g., to calculate percentage of column A in column B, format_options = {'of': A, 'in': B}
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: original dataframe with the new column added

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.sum_value_formatter
documentation:
Adds a new column with the summation of column A and column B
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: name of the new column
\:param columns: columns which are to be added
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: original dataframe with the new column added

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.date_diff_formatter
documentation:
Calculates the number of days between two date columns and inserts the difference in a new column
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: name of the new column
\:param format_options: list containing containing column names of from_date, to_date and date_format
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments, not required by this formatter
\:return: original dataframe with the new column added

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.bucket_formatter
documentation:
Convert integers into a user-defined labelled ranges. This formatter modifies the existing column.
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: column name to be modifies
\:param config: a dictionary containing list of range edges and labels of buckets
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments, not used in this formatter
\:return: dataframe with 'col_name' column modified as per the ranges

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.arithmetic_calculation_formatter
documentation:
Modifies the value of column or generates a new column with mathematically calculated value
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: name of the new column
\:param format_options : contains columns and operation
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: updated dataframe with the modified column

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.fill_empty_values
documentation:
Fill NA values in column 'col_name' with values taken from another column
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: column name on which the formatter is applied
\:param format_options: dictionary with a single key called 'column' representing
the column name which will be used to fill NA values
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: new dataframe with NA values replaced in column 'col_name'

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.fill_empty_values_with_custom_value
documentation:
NaN rows are filled with constant values
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: column name on which the formatter is applied
\:param format_options: dictionary key called value (representing
the values to fill)
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: new dataframe with NA values replaced in column 'col_name' with the 'value'

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.business_day_formatter
documentation:
returns billing date, i.e., previous business day for a every given date
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param new_col_name: column created after applying formatter
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: dataframe with a new column called billing_date for every entry of date column.

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.split_column_formatter
documentation:
Adds new columns with values from the given column
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: name of the column to be splitted
\:param format_options: new column names
\:param runtime_params: Not required for this formatter
\:return: dataframe with new columns having values obtained by splitting original column

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.last_date_of_prev_month
documentation:
This formatter adds a new column which contains last date of last month based on the rumtime date provided by the user.
the format options contain format of runtime date and output date, which is an input from the user
col_name is the name of the newly added column

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.conditional_replace_formatter
documentation:
selected rows are filled with column value mentioned
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: column name on which the formatter is applied
\:param format_options: dictionary key called value (representing
the values to fill)
\:param runtime_params: command line arguments
\:return: new dataframe with values replaced in column 'col_name' with the 'from_column'

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.replace_value
documentation:
values are replaced with the value specified
\:param dataframe: original dataframe
\:param col_name: column name on which the formatter is applied
\:param format_options: dictionary key called value (representing the from and to value)

> ```
> param runtime_params
>
> command line arguments
> ```

```
return

new dataframe with 'from_value' replaced in column 'col_name' with the 'to_value'
```

extract first value from matched pattern

add current timestamp value to a column

documentation:
create a new row with runtime date value

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.add_uuid_col
documentation:
Adds a new column with UUIDs
\:param dataframe: original dataframe

\:param format_options: None

get a substring from the column value

functions.name: ingen.formatters.common_formatters.float_precision
documentation:
get floating column precision by certain decimal after point

drop duplicate rows

Concatenates the provided columns, appends the string and adds as a new column to the dataframe

method responsible for calling the appropriate function to execute the process

documentation:
Merge two dataframes

\:param right_key: column name to join on in right dataframe

\:return: merged dataframe

convert columns to rows

key_column: Name to use for the ‘variable’ column
value_column: Name to use for the ‘value’ column

---
param params: has the command line arguments used while invoking

methods.name: ingen.generators.base_interface_generator.BaseInterfaceGenerator.generate
documentation:
Template method that defines the skeleton of interface generation
\:param interface_name: name of the interface, used for logging purpose
\:param sources: datasource definitions
\:param pre_processes: pre_processing steps, to be executed on the dataframe(s)
\:param columns: defines the columns and their formatting options
\:param destination: defines the destination parameters
\:param params: has the command line arguments used while invoking
\:param validation_action: Defines the validation action in the form of sending email in case of validation failure

functions.name: ingen.writer.json_util.json_sum
documentation:
Calculate the sum of a given collection in simple list,
dict with array, dict with field and subfield as data
\:param obj: The collection object to be used for sum.
\:param field: Optional param, mandatory if passing a dict of format {'key': [1,2,3..]}
\:param subfield: Optional param, mandatory only if passing dict of format {'key':'value', 'key2': [{'key3':'value'}, {'key3':'value'}]}
\:param result: Optional param, mandatory if using dict mode.
\:return: float sum if using array mode, dict gets a column added which name is passed as result.

modules.name: ingen.writer.util
documentation:
"
Utility class to add on custom functions for adding user defined header and footer as required

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_custom_value
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param props SQL query
\:param df dataframe object of records stored in output file.
\:param run_date date used via command line while invoking
\:return: custom header/footer as a string in the order defined in yml file.

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_constant
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param value : constant string
\:return: return the constant value as a string

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_filler
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param white_spaces: number of white spaces intended in header/footer
\:return: a string with white spaces equivalent to white_spaces

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_date
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param date_format : format of date required
\:return: date as a string in format as date_format

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_row_count
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param row_count_prop: dictionary containing values to calculate row count of the output file.
\:return: count of records in output file with left_padding which will right allign the result and fills
remaining space on left side with 0.
header: represents the number of lines the user wants to add from the header to the total row count.

> if header = bool & True:
>
> : row count= row_count +1
>
> else:
>
> : row count= row_count+ header(int value)

footer: represents the number of lines the user wants to add from the footer to the total row count.
: if footer = bool & True:

  : row count= row_count +1

  else:

  : row count= row_count+ footer(int value)

functions.name: ingen.writer.util.get_run_date
documentation:
Evaluates the values corresponding to the functions defined in yml and return the custom footer/header as a string.
\:param run_date_format: format of date required
\:return: run_date of the file in the format specified.

functions.name: ingen.utils.utils.holiday_calendar
documentation:
A utility method to get holiday calendar for country configured in property_instance file for given calendar year.

Returns HolidayBase object which is a dict like object

The key of the object is the date of the holiday and the value is the name
of the holiday itself. When passing the date as a key, the date can be
expressed as one of the following formats:
\* {class}`datetime.date`,
\* {class}`datetime.datetime`,
\* a {class}`str` of any format recognized by {func}`dateutil.parser.parse`,
\* or a {class}`float` or {class}`int` representing a POSIX timestamp.

functions.name: ingen.utils.utils.get_business_day
documentation:
A utility method to get business date for passed date.
If passed date is a public holiday, get previous or next business day
depending upon if next or prev is passed

classes.name: ingen.utils.utils.KeyValue
documentation:
A utility class to create dict-like key value pairs from command lines arguments

e.g, Following command lines argument
--query_params date=12/09/1995 name=merry table=position

will be converted into a dictionary
query_params = { 'date': '12/09/1995', 'name': 'merry', 'table': 'position }

Parses the SQL query string and returns the actual query.

Read the file based on file parmas

Parse the temp table config, read the config from file , insert the data from file to temp table and return a
list of queries

methods.name: ingen.utils.sql_query_parser.SqlQueryParser.create_temp_table
documentation:
Parse the temp table params, return a query string

Helper class to parse dynamic SQL queries, and replace the dynamic parts of the queries with their actual values
provided in either command line parameters or via a file.

functions.name: ingen.utils.app_http.aiohttp_retry.http_retry_request
documentation:
Asynchronously retries the HTTP call until the given success_criteria (a callable) is succeeded or
number of retries reaches to zero
\:param session: aiohttp ClientSession
\:param method: HTTP Methods
\:param url: Request URL
\:param retries: How many times to retry
\:param interval: How long to wait before retrying (in seconds)
\:param interval_increment: Number of seconds added to interval when retrying.

> So if a request fails with following params:
> retires = 4, interval = 2, interval_increment = 2
> 4 retries will be made after 2, 4, 6, and 8 seconds.

```
param success_criteria

a callable that defines the success_criteria,
see 'src.utils.app_http.success_criterias.py' for more

param criteria_options

dict-like options required by success_criteria method

param kwargs

additional kwargs for HTTP methods, eg., headers, auth, data etc

return

If successful, returns a namedtuple HTTPResponse containing response status, headers and body,
otherwise None
```

modules.name: ingen.utils.app_http.success_criterias
documentation:
HTTP Success Criteria

This module contains the functions that can be used to define success of a
HTTP response. Every function takes two inputs -
1. HTTPResponse - a namedtuple with following fields
    status:     HTTP status code
    headers:    HTTP response headers
    data:       HTTP response data

2. options - a dict containing criteria specific fields

The function should return True if the defined criteria is met by the given
HTTP response, otherwise False.

functions.name: ingen.utils.app_http.success_criterias.payload_criteria
documentation:
Checks if a given field is present in the response and is equal to the provided value
and there's no 'error' field in the response
\:param response: HTTPResponse namedtuple
\:param options: an options dict containing following keys:

> options[key] = Field name in the response
> options[value] = Expected value of 'data[key]'

```
return

True if 'key' is present in data and 'data[key] == value', otherwise False
```

functions.name: ingen.utils.app_http.success_criterias.status_criteria
documentation:
Checks if the response status code is equal to the expected code passed in options
\:param response: HTTPResponse namedtuple
\:param options: options[status_code] = HTTP status code
\:return: True or False

classes.name: ingen.utils.path_parser.PathParser

methods.name: ingen.utils.path_parser.PathParser.parse
documentation:
Replaces bfm tokens and dates in the file paths
\:param path: file path string. e.g, "\$token(FTP_DIR)/some/path/file_name\_\$date(%d%m%Y).csv"
\:return: path with tokens and date values. e.g, "u1/ftp/some/path/file_name_12092020.csv"

PathParser is a utility class that provides methods to parse file paths and replace
the dynamic fields in it with appropriate values. A replaceable item can be one of the following:
    1. date - $date(<FORMAT>) - it will be replaced by the date provided in the constructor in the given format

\:return: list of response body

\:param results: list of response body
\:return: None

functions.name: ingen.utils.app_http.http_util.api_auth

Method responsible for authenticating API. aiohttp.BasicAuth is used for it.
\:param auth: Dictionary that contains auth.type, auth.username and auth.pwd.
\:return: aiohttp.BasicAuth if auth.type is BasicAuth, None otherwise.

Accepts a string with dynamic tokens expressed as \$function_name(function_args) and returns a string with
the dynamic token replaced by their values. If 'function_name' is not found in the 'interpolator_function_map'
the string is returned as is.

classes.name: ingen.utils.url_constructor.UrlConstructor

methods.name: ingen.utils.url_constructor.UrlConstructor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize with a base url and a list of url params. Optionally, at most one param can be used to create a
batch of urls
\:param url: base url
\:param url_query_params: list of url params containing id and it's type
\:param batch: url_param id and batch_size
\:param run_date: run date passed via cmd line param

methods.name: ingen.utils.url_constructor.UrlConstructor.get_urls
documentation:
Constructs the URLs using the class variables and returns a list of URLs. If batching is off, list will contain
only one URL.
\:return: list of URLs

methods.name: ingen.utils.url_constructor.UrlConstructor.get_query_param_batch_urls
documentation:
Replace the given url param in the url and create a list of urls
\:param url: url string with replaceable param id within curly braces
\:param batch_id: id of url param
\:param batch_size: batch size
\:return: list of urls with id replaced with actual values

methods.name: ingen.utils.url_constructor.UrlConstructor.get_params
documentation:
Construct the url param string
\:return: url param string, eg, `?key1=value1&key2=value2`

A utility class to construct URLs with given base URL and its URL params. URL params can be fetched from a file,
a database or can be declared as a constant in the configuration file. More than one URL can be constructed to
support batching.
